{"dg-publish":true,"permalink":"/EXAM REVIEWS/SUBJECT/Bio 11/1.7 Animalia/","created":"2026-02-21T17:48:25.880-05:00","updated":"2026-04-05T18:22:01.068-04:00","dg-note-properties":{}}
---

2026-02-21 17:48

Status: #new
 
Tags: #biology #cram #kingdom
[[EXAM REVIEWS/SUBJECT/Bio 11/1.0e Eukaryote\|1.0e Eukaryote]]
# Animalia
#### Animal Details
- Eukaryotic and multicellular
- Do not contain cell walls
- Heterotrophs that ingest their food
- Are motile - require
- energy to move
- Reproduce sexually
#### Classifications
- 1. **Levels of organization
	- Animals can be differentiated based on tissues and organs
	
<div class="transclusion internal-embed is-loaded"><a class="markdown-embed-link" href="/Excalidraw/Cell Tissue Organ Organ System/" aria-label="Open link"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="svg-icon lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></a><div class="markdown-embed">




==⚠  Switch to EXCALIDRAW VIEW in the MORE OPTIONS menu of this document. ⚠== You can decompress Drawing data with the command palette: 'Decompress current Excalidraw file'. For more info check in plugin settings under 'Saving'


# Excalidraw Data

## Text Elements
Cells 
Tissue 
Organ 
Organ System 
Organism 


</div></div>

- 2. **Number of Body Layers
	- Animals develop from three layers of cells in the embryo
	<svg version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 498.18587777502245 240.53710937499994" width="498.18587777502245" height="240.53710937499994" class="excalidraw-svg"><!-- svg-source:excalidraw --><metadata></metadata><defs><symbol id="image-crop-90482998adce958a5012791edb8207b6a96e73d2-1323340549"><image href="data:image/png;base64," preserveAspectRatio="none" width="420.66222185863865" height="226.32812500000003"></image></symbol><style class="style-fonts">      @font-face { font-family: Excalifont; src: url(data:font/woff2;base64,); }</style></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="498.18587777502245" height="240.53710937499994" fill="#ffffff"></rect><mask id="mask-image-crop-sTFsA2bO" fill="#fff"><rect x="0" y="1.1445312500001052" width="421" height="221"></rect></mask><g mask="url(#mask-image-crop-sTFsA2bO)" transform="translate(10 8.855468749999895) rotate(0 210.33111092931932 111.41308593750007)"><use href="#image-crop-90482998adce958a5012791edb8207b6a96e73d2-1323340549" width="421" height="222.1445312500001" opacity="1"></use></g><g transform="translate(257.9756421793193 49.55212402343744) rotate(0 71.38873291015625 7.4892578125)"><text x="0" y="10.55685781250001" font-family="Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="11.98281250000001px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">Becomes skin and muscle</text></g><g transform="translate(259.9209546793193 133.94665527343744) rotate(0 41.52263641357422 7.489257812500007)"><text x="0" y="10.55685781250001" font-family="Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="11.98281250000001px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">Becomes spine</text></g><g transform="translate(261.9678296793193 188.77087402343744) rotate(0 113.10902404785156 7.489257812500006)"><text x="0" y="10.55685781250001" font-family="Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="11.98281250000001px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">Creates structural framework for body</text></g></svg>
	
- 3. Body Symmetry
	- Animals are either completely asymmetrical, radial or bilateral symmetrical
		![Pasted image 20260221221028.png](/img/user/7%20-%20Images/Pasted%20image%2020260221221028.png)
- 4. Body Cavity 
	- (Koiloma (cavity) Coelom (body cavity))
	- More complex organisms have a body cavity in which internal organs can develop
	- Essentially a empty space inside an organism
- 5. Segmentation
	- A segmented part of a body has many repetitive sections
		<svg version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 443.7890625 302.9169060202206" width="443.7890625" height="302.9169060202206" class="excalidraw-svg"><!-- svg-source:excalidraw 1.77,1.76 1.53,1.94 1.29,2.13 1.02,2.25 0.75,2.38 0.75,2.38 L 0.75,2.38 Z"></path></g><g transform="translate(25.974724264705742 249.32775160845597) rotate(0 198.93328857421875 21.794577205882327)"><text x="0" y="30.72163602941173" font-family="Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="34.871323529411725px" fill="#d3d3d3" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">Chordata Segmentation</text></g></svg>
- 6. Movement
	- Animals have at least one point in their development in which they are motile
	- ie. Coral zygotes swim to a suitable rock to settle in
- 7.  Reproduction
	- **All** animals are able to reproduce sexually. Some are able to reproduce asexually.

- # References
